meta.title: SIE DIVING | Santa Marta Expedition
meta.description: Join our next scuba diving expedition in Santa Marta. PADI Open Water and Advanced courses from June 13 to 18.

hero.title: Scuba Diving in Colombia
hero.subtitle: Explore the biodiversity of Providencia, Isla Fuerte, Gorgona, Malpelo, and Taganga. Elite training and unique expeditions in Colombia's best diving spots with SIE DIVING.

interest.title: Where to Scuba Dive in Colombia?
interest.desc: Colombia has some of the best diving sites in the world: Providencia, Isla Fuerte, Gorgona, Malpelo, and Taganga. SIE DIVING takes you to all of them. We build trust in your training and design experiences for certified divers in Colombia and the world.

features.title: Unique Biodiversity
features.desc: Encounter sea turtles, rays, and colorful schools of fish in their natural habitat.

features.title: PADI Instructors
features.desc: Learn with internationally certified professionals who prioritize your safety above all.

features.title: Crystal Clear Waters
features.desc: Enjoy exceptional visibility in the warm Caribbean Sea, year-round.

padi_ow.title: PADI Open Water
padi_ow.desc: Your passport to the underwater world. The most recognized scuba certification globally.

padi_adv.title: PADI Advanced
padi_adv.desc: Take your skills to the next level. Explore deep diving, navigation, and more.

action.title: Ready for the Adventure of a Lifetime?
action.desc: Spots for our Taganga expedition are limited. Secure your place now and get ready to discover a brand new world.

open_water.title: PADI Open Water Diver Course
open_water.subtitle: Your passport to the underwater world. The most recognized scuba certification globally.

modules.title: Knowledge Development
modules.desc: Learn the basic principles of scuba diving, safety concepts, and dive planning via PADI eLearning or classroom sessions.

modules.title: Confined Water Dives
modules.desc: Practice essential skills in a pool-like environment, including gear assembly, mask clearing, and buoyancy control.

modules.title: Open Water Dives
modules.desc: Complete 4 ocean dives (in Taganga) to demonstrate your skills and gain real-world experience exploring the sea.

prerequisites: Ability to swim and tread water for 10 minutes.

advanced.title: PADI Advanced Open Water Course
advanced.subtitle: Take your skills to the next level with 5 adventure dives. Less theory, more action.

required_modules.title: Deep Dive
required_modules.desc: Learn to plan and execute dives between 18 and 30 meters (60-100 ft) safely.

required_modules.title: Underwater Navigation
required_modules.desc: Improve your compass skills and learn to navigate using visual references and kick cycles.

elective_modules.title: Peak Performance Buoyancy
elective_modules.desc: Master your water control to dive effortlessly and conserve air.

elective_modules.title: Night Diving
elective_modules.desc: Discover how the reef comes alive at night and learn flashlight communication techniques.

elective_modules.title: Wreck Diving
elective_modules.desc: Learn the fundamentals of safely diving around sunken wrecks.

prerequisites: Certified as a PADI Open Water Diver (or equivalent).
At least 15 years old (12 for Junior Advanced).

equipment.title: Equipment for Safe Diving in Colombia
equipment.desc: Two oceans, two different realities. At SIE DIVING we select and maintain the right gear for the specific conditions of the Colombian Caribbean and Pacific.

items.title: Diving Mask
items.desc: Tempered double-lens glass and hypoallergenic silicone. A perfect seal is essential in Colombia's Caribbean and Pacific waters.

items.title: Buoyancy Control Device
items.desc: Controls buoyancy in changing waters. Essential for the strong currents of Malpelo and Gorgona in the Pacific.

items.title: First & Second Stage Regulator
items.desc: Your air source underwater. We use certified DIN regulators, checked before every dive on both coasts.

items.title: Wetsuit
items.desc: Caribbean (Taganga, Providencia): 3 mm · 26–29 °C. Pacific (Gorgona, Malpelo): 5–7 mm · 20–24 °C. Always the right thickness for each ocean.

items.title: Dive Computer
items.desc: Real-time monitoring of depth, time and no-decompression limits. Mandatory for technical Pacific dives.

items.title: Underwater Torch
items.desc: Reveals true colors below 10 m. Essential in Providencia's coral caves and the Pacific's dark depths.

items.title: SMB Surface Marker Buoy
items.desc: Signals your position when surfacing in open sea. Indispensable on ocean dives at Malpelo and Gorgona.

items.title: Propulsion Fins
items.desc: Blade or split fins depending on currents. Pacific currents demand more power than the warm Caribbean waters.
